claim: Context graphs are institutional memory of business decision rationale, built from decision traces capturing exceptions, overrides, and cross-system context
actionable: Model each business process as decision traces (steps + human overrides); aggregate them to build a context graph that agents can learn from
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital

claim: Context graphs are defensible moats for applied AI companies; they are unique, defensible, and poorly suited for incumbents
actionable: If building a systems-of-agents company, invest heavily in capturing decision traces from the start; this becomes your competitive advantage
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital

claim: Write path (operational decisions) captures context graphs better than read path (analytical systems); agents need operational context, not just end-state data
actionable: Build systems that capture decision sequences (not just outcomes); use operational traces, not just analytical warehousing, as your primary context source
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital

claim: Universal context graphs across enterprises will not emerge; context graphs are process-specific and will emerge organically for specific workflows
actionable: Do not attempt to build one unified context graph enterprise-wide; instead, build context graphs for specific business processes (e.g., Player Zero for code workflows)
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital

claim: Capturing partial why is possible by reconstructing intent from sequences of actions; LLMs make this feasible without manual structure
actionable: Use LLMs to analyze sequences of actions (queries, decisions, overrides) to reconstruct the reasoning; pair with optional human annotations for key decisions
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital

claim: Data governance and PII management are critical blockers; companies must implement data security infrastructure before operational context graphs
actionable: Before building context graphs, audit for sensitive data; use tools like Skyflow for PII detection; implement access controls and data lineage tracking
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital

claim: Context graphs are not equivalent to data meshes; they are not universal and do not require upfront ontology design like data meshes do
actionable: Treat context graphs as emergent from operational processes, not designed upfront; avoid data mesh trap of trying to unify all organizational data
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital

claim: Unstructured data (Zoom calls, emails, Slack) contains most value but is hardest to structure; small model layers extract signal from noise
actionable: Do not ingest entire unstructured sources; use small models to extract relevant decision context from emails, Slack, transcripts before adding to context graph
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital

claim: Context graph stack (analogous to modern data stack) will be well-defined within a year with agreed-upon best practices and infrastructure components
actionable: Anticipate that standardized tools and patterns for context graph implementation will emerge in 2026; plan to evaluate and adopt them
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital

claim: Automation is a staircase: more context graph insights → more automation → more decision traces → richer context graph → faster spiral
actionable: Use context graphs to identify automation opportunities; as you automate, capture more traces; iterate to accelerate the flywheel
source_title: Context Graphs: according to the authors — Jaya Gupta, Ashu Garg, Foundation Capital
